players.name: Matěj Kovář
players.bio: Young PSV stopper making his World Cup debut at 26. Kovář has been a reliable presence between the sticks in the Eredivisie and earned his spot as Czechia's number one through consistent club form.
players.facts: Born in 2000, Kovář is part of the 'millennium keeper' generation — too young for 2022, perfectly ripe for 2026.

players.name: David Zima
players.bio: A composed centre-back who returned home to Slavia Prague after stints in Italy. Zima reads the game well beyond his years and brings a rare calmness to the Czech backline at his first World Cup.
players.facts: Zima spent time at Torino in Serie A before returning to Slavia — an Italian education that sharpened his defensive instincts.

players.name: Tomáš Holeš
players.bio: The experienced Holeš is a rock at the back for Slavia Prague and Czechia alike. With 41 caps and two international goals, the 33-year-old brings leadership and grit to his first World Cup.
players.facts: Holeš can play defensive midfield too — a versatile Swiss army knife who quietly becomes indispensable in every squad he joins.

players.name: Robin Hranáč
players.bio: The Plzeň-born Hranáč moved to Bundesliga side Hoffenheim and quickly established himself as a commanding presence. At just 26, his World Cup debut marks a major step in a rapidly rising career.
players.facts: Hranáč and clubmate Coufal share a Hoffenheim dressing room — Czechia's own little colony in Baden-Württemberg.

players.name: Vladimír Coufal
players.bio: A seasoned right-back with over 60 caps, Coufal spent years at West Ham before heading to Hoffenheim. Known for his tireless overlapping runs and dependability, he's a true Czech international stalwart.
players.facts: Coufal once delivered a cross so precisely at West Ham that fans debated whether it was intentional. It was. Always is.

players.name: Štěpán Chaloupek
players.bio: At just 23, Chaloupek is one of the youngest defenders in this squad. The Slavia Prague centre-back has impressed domestically and brings youthful energy and ambition to his debut World Cup campaign.
players.facts: Born in Meziboří — a town so small that making a World Cup squad from there is statistically extraordinary.

players.name: Ladislav Krejčí
players.bio: The Prague-born midfielder has made a solid name for himself in the Premier League with Wolves. Energetic and technically gifted, Krejčí offers drive from midfield and chips in with important goals.
players.facts: Not to be confused with the other Ladislav Krejčí in Czech football — yes, there are two. Czechia keeps things interesting.

players.name: Vladimír Darida
players.bio: A Czech football institution with 79 caps, Darida has served his nation with distinction for over a decade. Now at Hradec Králové, the veteran midfield general brings World Cup experience and tactical savvy.
players.facts: Darida once won Czech Footballer of the Year — a fitting reward for a man who treats every training session like a final.

players.name: Adam Hložek
players.bio: One of Europe's most exciting young forwards, Hlože k burst onto the scene as a teenager and hasn't slowed down. Clinical, creative and calm under pressure — at 23, this World Cup could be his global coming-out party.
players.facts: Hlože k was one of the Bundesliga's most hyped teenagers at Bayer Leverkusen before moving to Hoffenheim to lead the line.

players.name: Patrik Schick
players.bio: Czechia's talisman and top scorer, Schick's 26 international goals speak for themselves. The Leverkusen striker is famous for that stunning long-range screamer against Scotland at Euro 2020 — a goal seen from orbit.
players.facts: Schick's goal vs Scotland at Euro 2020 from the halfway line was voted UEFA's Goal of the Tournament. Goalkeepers still have nightmares.

players.name: Jan Kuchta
players.bio: A bustling, physical striker who never gives defenders a moment's rest. Kuchta has been a reliable option for Czechia and Sparta Prague, offering work rate and an eye for goal as a squad player at this World Cup.
players.facts: Kuchta scored on his senior debut for Czechia — the kind of entrance that makes veteran teammates quietly reassess their career choices.

players.name: Lukáš Červ
players.bio: A dynamic central midfielder from Viktoria Plzeň, Červ has quickly impressed at international level. Industrious and technically sound, he represents the next generation of Czech midfield talent stepping up on the world stage.
players.facts: Červ means 'worm' in Czech — but there's nothing wriggly about his direct, purposeful style in the middle of the park.

players.name: Mojmír Chytil
players.bio: A sharp and mobile striker with an impressive six goals in just 22 caps, Chytil has proven himself a reliable finisher. The Slavia Prague forward brings competition and creativity to Czechia's forward line.
players.facts: Chytil's goals-per-cap ratio rivals some of Czech football's greatest forwards — a stat that makes him far from unlucky number 13.

players.name: David Jurásek
players.bio: An attack-minded left-back who loves to bomb forward, Jurásek has been a consistent performer for Slavia Prague and earned his international place through tireless displays. Already 18 caps at 25 — his ceiling is high.
players.facts: Jurásek hails from Dolní Němčí — a village so far off the map that GPS systems reportedly give up and suggest a detour.

players.name: Pavel Šulc
players.bio: The creative spark from Karlovy Vary swapped Viktoria Plzeň for Ligue 1's Lyon, bringing his flair to French football. Šulc has five international goals and the kind of technical quality that makes defenders nervous.
players.facts: Šulc moved to Lyon — a club so historic that even the training cones have more Champions League experience than some nations.

players.name: Jindřich Staněk
players.bio: The Strakonice-born shot-stopper has been a cornerstone of Slavia Prague's recent dominance and brings composure beyond his years. At 30, Staněk arrives at the World Cup as a proven European-level goalkeeper.
players.facts: Staněk has conceded penalty shootout agony at club level but remains unflappable — the hallmark of a keeper who simply never panics.

players.name: Lukáš Provod
players.bio: A versatile and energetic left winger, Provod has been a consistent performer for Slavia Prague and the national team. With 38 caps and a nose for big moments, he's a reliable weapon in Czechia's attacking armoury.
players.facts: Provod scored a memorable long-range goal for Slavia in European competition — proof that Czech midfielders don't just pass, they punish.

players.name: Michal Sadílek
players.bio: Born in Uherské Hradiště, Sadílek grew up in the Netherlands and represents a dual-culture blend of Czech grit and Dutch football philosophy. A versatile operator, he brings experience and intelligence on the left flank.
players.facts: Sadílek's father Michal Sr. also played football professionally — making them a bona fide Czech football dynasty, just a quieter one.

players.name: Tomáš Chorý
players.bio: Tall, physical and aerially dominant, Chorý is a traditional centre-forward who terrorises defences with his presence alone. Seven goals in 22 caps is a fine return for the Slavia Prague man at his first World Cup.
players.facts: At 6ft 4in, Chorý wins more aerial duels than most players have hot dinners. Defenders simply don't enjoy facing him.

players.name: Jaroslav Zelený
players.bio: A dependable left-back with over two decades of professional experience, Zelený brings maturity and solidity to the Czech backline. Now at Sparta Prague, the 33-year-old is a trusted senior figure in this squad.
players.facts: Zelený is one of only two players in this squad born in the early 1990s — old enough to remember Czechoslovakia, young enough to still bomb forward.

players.name: David Douděra
players.bio: An industrious right midfielder with a knack for intelligent movement, Doudéra has been one of Slavia Prague's most consistent performers. His international record is growing and the World Cup stage suits his ambitions.
players.facts: Doudéra hails from Brandýs nad Labem-Stará Boleslav — a town with a longer name than his career stat line, for now.

players.name: Tomáš Souček
players.bio: The beating heart of Czech football, Souček brings power, precision and goals from deep. With 90 caps and 17 international goals, the West Ham midfield colossus is arguably Czechia's greatest current player.
players.facts: Souček scored 10 Premier League goals in a single season for West Ham — making him statistically one of football's most lethal defensive midfielders ever.

players.name: Lukáš Horníček
players.bio: The youngest and least-capped goalkeeper in the squad, Horníček is at Braga in Portugal honing his craft. With just one cap, this World Cup is a remarkable milestone for the 23-year-old third-choice stopper.
players.facts: Horníček plays in Portugal for Braga — a club where saving the day in European nights is practically written into the contract.

players.name: Alexandr Sojka
players.bio: A promising young central midfielder from hometown club Viktoria Plzeň, Sojka has only two senior caps but has impressed enough to earn a World Cup spot. The 23-year-old is very much one for the future — and now.
players.facts: Sojka was born in Plzeň and plays for Plzeň — a man so loyal to his city he probably drinks nothing but Pilsner Urquell.

players.name: Hugo Sochůrek
players.bio: At just 18, Sochárek is the youngest player in the Czech squad and one of the youngest at the tournament. The Sparta Prague midfielder is a rare talent — earning a World Cup call-up before most players finish school.
players.facts: Born in 2008, Sochárek was still in primary school when Patrik Schick was already playing Serie A. Football generations are wild.

players.name: Denis Višinský
players.bio: An inventive attacking midfielder from Viktoria Plzeň, Višinský already has an international goal in just two caps — a stunning return. The 23-year-old Mělník native brings creativity and unpredictability off the bench.
players.facts: Višinský scored in just his second international appearance — not a bad way to announce yourself on the global stage.
